adds.rationale: Rocket Lab has demonstrated early-surge commercial momentum — first mid-air booster capture in May 2022 and record Q2 revenue of $55.5M (+392% YoY) confirm the company is in the buildup-to-surge phase of the rockets wave the investor profile names as the next wave after AI.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab's 'There And Back Again' mission on May 2, 2022 captured the returning Electron booster with a helicopter — first mid-air booster recovery attempt.
Rocket Lab Q2 2022 revenue of $55.5M, +392% YoY and +36% sequential, with Q3 guidance of $60-63M; backlog $545M.
RKLB commenced trading on Nasdaq on August 25, 2021, confirming >1yr of public price history as of this rebalance.

adds.rationale: Two independent structural catalysts landed before this rebalance — the EU Parliament's July 2022 endorsement of nuclear as a 'green' sustainable activity and the August 2022 Inflation Reduction Act's nuclear tax credits — marking the start of a policy-driven fission renaissance; NLR (VanEck Uranium and Nuclear ETF) provides diversified exposure across uranium miners, utilities, and reactor builders.

news_evidence.summary: EU included nuclear as a 'transitional' sustainable activity in its taxonomy; the EU Parliament rejected a motion opposing this classification in July 2022.
The Inflation Reduction Act, signed August 16, 2022, introduced a nuclear production tax credit up to $15/MWh for existing plants and a 30% ITC for new nuclear.
The IRA earmarked $700M for domestic HALEU production, addressing fuel-supply security after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

adds.rationale: North American robot orders hit consecutive quarterly records in early 2022 as manufacturers deployed automation to offset labor shortages, confirming the robotics wave is in its early-surge phase; BOTZ (Global X Robotics & AI ETF) is the most liquid single-ticker way to ride this theme.

news_evidence.summary: A3 reported Q1 2022 North American robot sales hit a record 11,595 units ($646M), up 28% in units and 43% in dollars YoY.
A3 data published May 31, 2022 confirmed North American robot sales continued the record surge into 2022, driven by labor shortages.
Bloomberg reported US robot orders jumped 28% in 2021 to an all-time high; robot-leasing models accelerating adoption among mid-size manufacturers.

rationale_overall: The watchlist grows from 5 to its maximum of 8 by adding one ticker per underrepresented next-wave bucket. RKLB fills rockets/spacecraft on its first reusable-booster capture and near-400% Q2 revenue growth. NLR fills nuclear at a strong policy-catalyst moment (EU green-taxonomy endorsement + IRA nuclear tax credits + European energy crisis). BOTZ fills robotics on consecutive record North American robot-order quarters. No removes: the five holdings are in a broad-market bear phase but none has suffered a thesis breach.
